subject: Statistics
title: Heavy Tails in Finance
description: Learn Heavy Tails in Finance in one minute.

hook.text: In 1998, Long-Term Capital Management lost $4.6 billion in weeks. Their models said it was impossible.

buildup.text: Financial models assumed stock returns were normal — most moves small, crashes almost never.

discovery.text: Real markets have fat tails. Extreme daily moves happen 10-100× more often than the bell curve says.

twist.text: The 2008 crisis and COVID crash were 'impossible' under normal models. Both happened anyway.

climax.text: Ignoring fat tails isn't theoretical. It causes real bankruptcies, bailouts, and global recessions.

punchline.text: Models that ignore fat tails create the next crisis.

quiz.question: Why did financial models fail in 2008?
options: They assumed normal distributions — underestimating the probability of extreme events
They only looked at one year of history
